scope: TG-P9 terminal: pure generated 8B decode-attention primitive route
north_star: TINYGRAD_DEFAULT_PURITY_PASS remains blocked, but the final impurity is now pinned to ONE concrete compiler primitive gap (not 'attention is slower').

phase_results.TG_P9_1: PASS_LIVE_TC_SPLIT_IR (the live-split geometry TG-P8 expected EMITTER_BLOCKED is expressible in generated UOp; a typed-const bug, not a capability gap)
phase_results.TG_P9_2: PASS_LIVE_SPLIT_TILE (live-split tile byte-identical; ctx512 tile 36.6->8.0us, 4.6x; full-model ctx512 87.7%->96.7% of owned, token-identical, route-bound)
phase_results.TG_P9_3_4: BLOCKED_EMITTER (split-preserving combine: every weight-sharing/gmax-fusing shape mis-vectorizes the reduction-accumulator REG to a non-assignable make_float4(...) = ...; REG_STORE_DEVEC=1 compiles but NaNs). The per-d fexp redundancy -- the ctx4096 556us cap -- cannot be removed in current AMD codegen.
phase_results.TG_P9_5: REFUTE_STILL_SLOW (full candidate = live-split tile + existing combine: ctx512 96.7%, ctx4096 95.3%; improved from 87.7%/95.9% but <98% at both -- combine-capped)

live_split_tile.note: closes the ctx512 SPLIT_GEOMETRY half of the TG-P8 blocker; byte-identical, route-bound

remaining_blocker.what: a split-preserving generated LSE combine that de-duplicates the softmax-weight fexp across d (or fuses the gmax max-reduce) so the combine lifecycle drops from ~763us/fwd to ~owned 224us
remaining_blocker.why: tinygrad AMD codegen vectorizes the reduction-accumulator REG into a non-assignable make_float4(...) store for any combine shape beyond the shipped single-reduce per-d combine; REG_STORE_DEVEC=1 compiles but the max-reduce mis-lowers to NaN
remaining_blocker.reopen_condition: a tinygrad codegen fix keeping the reduction-accumulator REG scalar (or a correct DEVEC max-reduce lowering) for a multi-reduce / LDS-weight-sharing combine kernel; then re-run TG-P9.4/9.5
